benchmarks.title: IEEE 1584-2018 Arc Flash — Example 1 (MV Open Air)
benchmarks.description: Medium-voltage open-air bus, bolted fault 10 kA, 15 kV, 18-inch working distance.

benchmarks.title: IEC 60909-0:2016 Short-Circuit — Three-Phase Maximum Current
benchmarks.description: 110 kV / 10 kV transformer network. Known I"k3, peak current ip, thermal equivalent Ith from IEC 60909 §4 example.

benchmarks.title: IEC 60287-1-1:2023 Cable Ampacity — Direct Burial, 3-Core XLPE
benchmarks.clause: §2.1 — Continuous current rating, direct buried installation
benchmarks.description: 95 mm² Cu XLPE 11 kV, three-core cable directly buried 800 mm depth, soil ρ = 1.0 K·m/W, ambient 25 °C.

benchmarks.title: IEEE 80-2013 Ground Grid — Rectangular Mesh, Resistivity 100 Ω·m
benchmarks.description: 70 × 70 m grid, 12 meshes × 12 meshes, 100 Ω·m soil, 3 kA fault current, 1 s clearing time.

benchmarks.title: Heat Trace Screening — Freeze Protection, Water Pipe, Above Ground
benchmarks.description: 2-inch nominal steel pipe, water service, maintain 5 °C, ambient −20 °C, 25 mm mineral wool insulation.
benchmarks.reference: nVent TraceCalc method; IEC 62395-2 §5 screening calculation

benchmarks.title: Duct Bank Thermal — Neher-McGrath, 4 Conduits, 5 Cables per Duct
benchmarks.clause: §6 — Duct bank thermal resistance, group derating
benchmarks.description: 2 × 2 duct bank, PVC conduit, backfill ρ = 0.9 K·m/W, ambient 25 °C, 350 kcmil Cu XHHW, 90% load factor.
benchmarks.reference: IEEE 835-1994 Table B-75; ETAP Underground Raceway Systems validation

standards.name: IEEE 1584-2018
standards.title: Guide for Performing Arc-Flash Hazard Calculations
standards.scope: Arc flash incident energy and protection boundary for AC equipment 208 V – 15 kV
assumptions: Arcing faults modeled as bolted fault with empirical reduction factor
Incident energy assumes person at working distance perpendicular to the arc
Three-phase balanced system; unbalanced faults not covered
limitations: Validated for 208 V to 15 kV; extrapolation outside this range is not supported
Does not cover DC arc flash (use NFPA 70E Annex D.8 or IEC 62271)

standards.name: IEC 60909-0:2016
standards.title: Short-circuit currents in three-phase AC systems — Calculation of currents
standards.scope: Maximum and minimum short-circuit currents for three-phase AC systems up to 550 kV
assumptions: Equivalent voltage source method (no pre-fault load currents in calculation)
Voltage factor c applied to nominal voltage (1.0–1.1 for max; 0.95 for min)
Far-from-generator condition unless generator correction factor K_G is applied
limitations: Near-to-generator μ factor (Ib reduction) not yet implemented
Transformer correction factor K_T exported but not batch-applied per transformer
Generator correction factor K_G not implemented

standards.name: IEC 60287-1-1:2023
standards.title: Electric cables — Calculation of the current rating
standards.scope: Continuous current ratings for cables in direct burial, conduit, trays, and free air
assumptions: 100% load factor (continuous steady-state rating)
Uniform soil temperature and resistivity along cable route
Cables in trefoil or flat formation (no transposition modeled)
limitations: Cyclic/daily load profiles not yet supported (use IEC 60287-2-2 method)
Soil drying-out (critical isotherm) model not implemented
Cable crossings and mutual heating from parallel routes require manual input

standards.name: IEEE 80-2013
standards.title: Guide for Safety in AC Substation Grounding
standards.scope: Rectangular ground grid design: mesh voltage, step voltage, grid resistance
assumptions: Uniform soil resistivity (single-layer model)
Rectangular grid geometry; irregular shapes require mesh-boundary approximation
Touch/step voltage limits from IEEE 80 Table 7 (heart-current factor K_h = 1.0)
limitations: Two-layer soil model not yet implemented (use SES CDEGS for multi-layer analysis)
Irregular electrode geometry (L-shaped grids, remote ground rods) approximated
Transferred voltage check not automated

standards.name: IEC 61000-4-15:2010+AMD1:2023
standards.title: Flickermeter — Functional and design specifications
standards.scope: Short-term (Pst) and long-term (Plt) flicker severity at the PCC
assumptions: Simplified rectangular voltage-change method (IEC 61000-4-15 Annex A)
Thevenin short-circuit impedance at PCC: ΔV% = ΔP_kW / S_sc_kVA × 100
Pst look-up from IEC 61000-4-15 Figure A.1 iso-Pst contours via bilinear interpolation
limitations: Time-domain flickermeter waveform simulation not implemented (IEC 61000-4-15 §4)
Only rectangular voltage changes modeled; ramp or sinusoidal changes not supported
IEEE 1453 North American 120 V / 60 Hz weighting curves not separately validated

standards.name: IEEE 835-1994 / Neher-McGrath (1957)
standards.title: Standard Power Cable Ampacity Tables / Duct Bank Thermal Method
standards.scope: Duct bank thermal resistance and ampacity derating for underground cable systems
assumptions: Steady-state thermal equilibrium
Soil thermal resistivity uniform around duct bank
Cables uniformly loaded across all ducts (no mixed loading)
limitations: Cyclic load profiles not modeled
Mutual heating from crossing cables or adjacent duct banks not automated
Backfill thermal runaway (soil dry-out) requires manual critical isotherm check

standards.name: IEEE 1584-2018
standards.title: Guide for Performing Arc-Flash Hazard Calculations (2018 revision)
standards.scope: Replaces 2002 edition; covers 208 V–15 kV, all equipment configurations

standards.name: NFPA 70E-2024
standards.title: Standard for Electrical Safety in the Workplace
standards.scope: PPE selection from incident energy and arc flash boundary per IEEE 1584
assumptions: PPE levels based on cal/cm² threshold per Table 130.5(G)
limitations: PPE category method (Table 130.7(C)(15)) not automated — use incident energy method

standards.name: NFPA 70 (NEC) 2023 - Selected Ampacity Checks
standards.title: Selected NEC 2023 Article 310 ampacity references / Neher-McGrath duct bank method
standards.scope: Selected North American conductor ampacity references and duct bank derating factors used by the cable schedule, auto-size, and duct bank workflows. This is not a full NEC compliance engine.
assumptions: Pre-computed values at 30 C ambient, 75 C or 90 C conductor temperature
Selected installation conditions entered by the user are used for ambient, bundling, tray, or duct bank derating
limitations: Terminal temperature limitations, conductor insulation/listing details, and equipment listing requirements require engineer/AHJ review
Local amendments, special occupancies, installation exceptions, and complete Chapter 9 raceway details are not fully modeled
Tabulated values and screening calculations do not replace a stamped NFPA 70 design review

standards.name: IEEE 515-2017
standards.title: Standard for the Testing, Design, Installation, and Maintenance of Electrical Resistance Heat Tracing for Industrial Applications
standards.scope: Freeze protection and process temperature maintenance for industrial pipe heat tracing
assumptions: Steady-state heat balance: heat input = heat loss through insulation
Simplified cylindrical insulation model (ln(r_outer/r_pipe) thermal resistance)
limitations: Startup power (cold cable inrush) not modeled in screening calculation
Hazardous area derating factors require manual input
Self-regulating vs. constant-watt power-temperature curves not distinguished in screening

standards.name: ANSI/IEEE C37.010-2016
standards.title: Application Guide for AC High-Voltage Circuit Breakers
standards.scope: ANSI short-circuit multiplying factors for 1/2-cycle and 30-cycle networks
assumptions: Symmetrical 1/2-cycle fault current with ANSI multiplying factors (MF = 1.0–1.6)
Motor contribution modeled as fixed impedance (4 × motor kVA base)
limitations: Asymmetrical first-cycle peak current calculated with empirical MF, not κ (use IEC 60909 for κ method)
Generator near-to-fault decay not modeled in simplified ANSI method

standards.name: IEEE 80-2013 / ASTM G57
standards.title: IEEE 80 Advanced — Two-Layer Soil Wenner Fit, Polygon Grid, Hazard Map
standards.clause: §12.4 — Effective soil resistivity; Sunde (1968) image method
standards.description: Synthetic two-layer soil with ρ₁ = 100 Ω·m, ρ₂ = 400 Ω·m, h = 3 m. Seven Wenner measurements (a = 1–30 m). Expected: fitted ρ₁ within 5 % of 100, fitted ρ₂ within 10 % of 400, fitted h within 15 % of 3 m. Rectangular polygon (20 × 15 m) screening check: GPR, mesh voltage, and step voltage consistent with analyzeGroundGrid baseline.
standards.reference: Sunde (1968) Earth Conduction Effects §3-3; ASTM G57 §7; IEEE 80-2013 §12.4

standards.name: IEEE 399-1997
standards.title: Recommended Practice for Industrial and Commercial Power System Analysis (Brown Book)
standards.scope: Newton-Raphson load flow for industrial power systems
assumptions: Balanced three-phase system in per-unit on common MVA base
PQ (load) buses and PV (generator) buses; one slack bus required
Convergence tolerance 1 × 10⁻⁶ pu mismatch
limitations: Unbalanced (unsymmetrical) load flow not supported
DC power flow approximation not available

necComplianceMatrix.nec2023Scope: Publishes selected NFPA 70 (NEC) 2023 references, assumptions, limitations, and module-by-module scope.
implemented: Supported standards metadata
Benchmark and automated-test evidence
Explicit NEC 2023 scope matrix
gaps: Does not certify the full application as NFPA 70 compliant
Does not model local amendments, AHJ interpretations, equipment listings, or construction-document signoff
necComplianceMatrix.nextAction: Keep NEC language scoped to selected checks and route final compliance decisions to engineer/AHJ review.

necComplianceMatrix.nec2023Scope: Screens feeders, branch circuits, motor branch circuits, transformer OCPD, selected standard OCPD sizes, and conductor ampacity using selected NEC 2023 references.
implemented: Continuous-load factor for selected feeder/branch sizing
Selected standard OCPD sizes
NEC Table 310.16 baseline conductor ampacity with ambient, bundling, and tray factors
Selected NEC 110.14(C) terminal-temperature cap handling for feeder, motor, and transformer conductor sizing
Selected motor FLC and transformer sizing references
gaps: Conductor/listing details, equipment-specific listing constraints, and all NEC 110.14(C) conditions are not fully enforced
Does not implement the complete NEC Article 240, 430, or 450 decision trees
Does not cover every conductor type, insulation, wiring method, occupancy, or AHJ amendment
necComplianceMatrix.nextAction: Use as a sizing aid, then verify final conductor, OCPD, terminal temperature, and listing constraints in the construction design.

necComplianceMatrix.nec2023Scope: Captures load records and selected demand/schedule calculations that support NEC Article 220 style design workflows.
implemented: Structured load records by source, voltage, phase, demand, and power factor
Demand Schedule profiles for commercial/non-dwelling, industrial/process, dwelling, healthcare, and marina/shore-power screening
Profile review notes that keep unsupported occupancy-specific categories at 100 percent demand instead of silently applying unrelated reductions
Workflow links from loads into one-line, cable schedule, and downstream studies
gaps: Full NEC Article 220 service/feeder demand calculation coverage is not complete
Special occupancy, dwelling, healthcare, EV, industrial process, marina/shore-power, and local amendment rules require manual review
Demand factors entered by users are not a substitute for project-specific code classification
necComplianceMatrix.nextAction: Add detailed occupancy-specific Article 220 calculation worksheets before presenting demand scheduling as a full NEC compliance workflow.

necComplianceMatrix.nec2023Scope: Screens cable voltage drop against NEC informational-note recommendations for feeder, branch, and combined paths.
implemented: 3 percent feeder recommendation
3 percent branch-circuit recommendation
5 percent combined feeder-plus-branch recommendation metadata
Project-wide cable schedule scan
gaps: NEC voltage-drop text is informational-note guidance, not an enforceable pass/fail code limit by itself
Combined upstream-plus-downstream path aggregation is not fully modeled
The primary study is a screening method and does not replace impedance-method verification where reactance and power factor matter
necComplianceMatrix.nextAction: Label results as recommendation status and add explicit feeder-plus-branch path aggregation before treating combined results as complete.

necComplianceMatrix.nec2023Scope: Screens selected cable tray and conduit fill conditions for tray/raceway planning.
implemented: Cable tray fill percentage checks in routing and reporting
Conduit/tray fill calculators and over-limit warnings
Design Rule Checker DRC-10 screens assigned conduit fill from conduit type, trade size, cable OD/area, and selected Chapter 9 Table 1 limits
Cable schedule routing readiness indicators
gaps: Conduit Chapter 9 conditions, conductor insulation details, nipples, multiconductor cable rules, and all raceway types are not fully modeled across every page
Project report conduit area estimates are screening values unless sourced from verified conduit data
Tray fill limits vary by cable type, installation, and Article 392 condition and require final review
necComplianceMatrix.nextAction: Unify DRC-10 conduit-fill basis with fill pages, reports, ductbanks, and routing before claiming complete NEC raceway fill coverage.

necComplianceMatrix.nec2023Scope: Checks selected cable tray, conduit fill, ampacity derating, grounding presence and selected sizing, segregation, routing, and parallel-conductor issues.
implemented: Selected tray fill warnings
Selected ampacity derating warnings
Equipment grounding conductor presence warning
Selected copper equipment grounding conductor sizing checks from OCPD rating
Selected conductor/OCPD protection checks for NEC 240.4(D), 240.6(A), and terminal-temperature ampacity screening
Selected conduit fill screening using NEC Chapter 9 Table 1 limits when conduit/raceway assignments and cable OD/area data are available
Parallel conductor minimum-size and equal-length data warnings
gaps: Equipment grounding conductor sizing currently covers selected copper sizing breakpoints and common OCPD fields; alternate materials, upsized phase conductors, parallel EGCs, and special cases require final review
Conductor/OCPD checks are conservative screening only and do not implement every next-size, tap, motor, HVAC, transformer, feeder, or equipment-specific exception
Conduit fill checks are selected screening only and do not implement every Chapter 9 note, nipple condition, all insulation/raceway variations, or ductbank-specific path condition
Does not implement complete neutral, adjustment, correction, exception, or conductor-count logic
Findings are quality gates and review prompts, not a full NEC plan-check
necComplianceMatrix.nextAction: Expand conductor/OCPD exception handling, conduit-fill edge cases, and rule-specific basis notes before expanding DRC language beyond selected checks.

necComplianceMatrix.nec2023Scope: Screens equipment interrupting rating, withstand, and short-circuit duty data used in NEC 110 and equipment evaluation workflows.
implemented: AIC/SCCR-style rating comparisons
Equipment duty warnings from available fault-current data
gaps: Does not perform complete NEC 110, 409, listing, labeling, field-marking, or selective coordination review
Equipment data quality depends on user-entered ratings and upstream study assumptions
necComplianceMatrix.nextAction: Keep results framed as equipment-duty screening until equipment-listing and field-label workflows are complete.

necComplianceMatrix.nec2023Scope: Screens equipment compatibility with entered hazardous-area classification data.
implemented: Class/division and zone-oriented compatibility checks
Warnings for protection type, group, and temperature-rating conflicts
gaps: Does not perform full NEC Articles 500 through 506 wiring-method, seal, marking, or classification-boundary design
Area classification inputs and equipment certificates require specialist review
necComplianceMatrix.nextAction: Treat as a hazardous-area screening aid until classification workflow, seal requirements, and wiring-method checks are expanded.
